meta.scoreMethod: profitScore and openScienceScore are computed internal metrics, not factual fields. All factual financial/business-model claims should be read via provenance.fieldSources, provenance.materialClaims, and financialSummaryStrict.

confidenceLevels.high: Key legal/financial facts verified from official or filing-grade sources.
confidenceLevels.medium: Core model verified from official website or credible source, but some details remain unverified or only secondarily sourced.
confidenceLevels.low: Identity or product exists, but financial/legal specifics remain largely unresolved.

meta.disclaimer: Do not compare fundingRaised across nodes without reading provenance.fieldStatus and financialSummaryStrict. Even after cleanup, some entities disclose treasuries, grants, market cap, or AUM rather than directly comparable startup fundraising.
meta.secondPassNote: Low-confidence nodes were re-checked against official sites, docs, GitHub orgs, and credible announcements; many moved to medium/high, while opaque projects remain low.

scoring.profitScoreDescription: 0 = commons/nonprofit orientation, 100 = strongly commercial / investor- or fee-driven orientation.
scoring.openScienceCompatibilityDescription: 0 = poor fit with open-science-compatible business models, 100 = strong fit with community/open infrastructure norms.

meta.attributionPolicy: pass7-source-audit: every node now includes per-field source mapping, a source-backed strict summary, and conservative wording when financing, legal wrapper, or treasury figures are not explicitly disclosed in primary sources. Unsupported specifics were removed or downgraded.
